observations.summary: Phantom halts recur for a 4th consecutive story (4.1, 4.2, 4.3, 4.6): implement-story fired 2 halts with outcomeHistory ["COMPLETE"] and no question in the responseExcerpt; traces are now enabled and confirm the agent never posed a question — the halt is a machinery artifact, not a genuine decision escalation.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for implement-story (halts: 2, outcomeHistory: ["COMPLETE"], responseExcerpt is a normal implementation summary with no question). traces/4.6-a1-20260713T093042/implement-story.json — 541 parts; default view shows coherent completion summary; --grep for HALT/question returns no matches in text parts; --include machinery --grep for halt/QUESTION/reason returns no matches; --narrative (65 of 541 parts) shows a coherent flow with no question or decision escalation. No runner-errors.jsonl entry for this run. Normal duration (2592850ms vs 2037450ms average). Ledger entries 70 (4.1, hypothesized: 'The halts are machinery artifacts rather than genuine decision escalations' with nextStep: 'Enabling trace recording for the next run would allow widening into the mid-stream conversation'), 75 (4.2, confirmed), 82 (4.3, confirmed: 'traces are still not enabled, preventing root cause determination') — traces are now enabled for 4.6, and the prior hypothesis is confirmed: no question was posed.

observations.summary: The 4.3 update_step to implement-story is working — review-tests found 0 stale transitional markers for the first time in 10+ stories; Story 4.6's own test file was clean with no red-phase or green-phase claims, and implement-story correctly removed all transitional markers during the REFACTOR phase.
observations.evidence: Journal step_end for review-tests: 'Stale Transitional Markers — 0 FOUND (nothing to fix). Every comment/header claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase was verified against the actual test state. All markers are accurate.' _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-review-validation-report-4-6.md:133 — '0 stale transitional markers found.' test-review-validation-report-4-6.md:54-56 — Story 4.6's test file (deploy-workflow.spec.ts): 'The file header was cleaned during the REFACTOR phase. The current header contains no transitional markers.' Journal step_end for implement-story: 'All 31 ATDD tests un-skipped and passing. Transitional phase markers removed from test file and ATDD checklist.' Playbook implement-story prompt (updatedAt: 2026-07-12T15:50:56.335Z, updatedByRun: 4.3-a1-20260712T082910) includes 'remove all transitional phase markers from test-file headers and test planning artifacts (such as ATDD checklists) — any comment, header, or status line that describes a TDD phase transition (whether claiming tests are skipped, disabled, in a red-phase, or in a green-phase state) must be removed entirely rather than relabeled.' Ledger entries 71 (4.1), 77 (4.2), 84 (4.3) record prior occurrences; ledger entry 86 (4.3) records the applied update_step.

observations.summary: 2 always-skipped E2E tests from Story 1.3 (onboarding.spec.ts:232,284) are flagged for removal but not removed — 3rd occurrence (3.3, 3.4, 4.6) of the same dead tests persisting because the review-tests prompt says 'flag' not 'remove directly' for skipped tests with real assertions.
observations.evidence: _bmad-output/test-artifacts/test-review-validation-report-4-6.md:91-104 — 2 always-skipped E2E tests (onboarding.spec.ts:232,284 from Story 1.3) flagged for removal: 'FLAG for removal — permanently dead tests. Alternative: convert to conditional skips.' Line 104: 'Reason for flagging rather than removing directly: These tests are from Story 1.3, not Story 4.6. The user instruction to remove them directly wherever found during the search applies to empty placeholder test stubs (tests with no assertions), not to skipped tests with real assertions.' Ledger entry 19 (3.3): '8 test.skip() calls in Playwright E2E tests from stories 1.2, 1.3, and 2.3 persist in the codebase; no step ever resolves them.' Ledger entry 22 (3.4): '3 out-of-scope Playwright skips from Stories 1.2/1.3 persist and are deferred per DP-5 on every run.' The same 2 onboarding.spec.ts tests persist from 3.3 through 4.6 (6 of the original 8 were converted to conditional skips in intervening runs; 2 remain always-skipped). Playbook review-tests prompt says 'Flag skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal (with reason)' — 'flag' not 'remove directly', unlike stale markers ('Fix directly') and empty stubs ('remove them directly').

observations.summary: Recurrence: prepare-tests created incomplete regression guards for the deploy workflow — the permissions test checks that actions and contents are read but does not verify no other scopes exist, and the quality-gate command test only verifies the workflow flag while 5 critical flags (--status, --limit, --json, --repo, --branch) have no assertions; the 3.10 update_step directed applying the guard template and the agent did, but the template covers only credential-isolation and input-injection invariants, not permissions-scope completeness or command-argument coverage.
observations.evidence: _bmad-output/test-artifacts/nfr-assessment-4-6.md:71 — 'Permissions: test verifies actions and contents are read. But test does NOT verify these are the ONLY permissions — excess permissions (e.g., packages: write) could be added without detection.' nfr-assessment-4-6.md:72 — 'Deployability: quality-gate command flags (--status=completed, --limit=1, --json, --repo, --branch=) are not fully tested — removing any flag would weaken the quality gate silently.' Story file _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/4-6-add-the-manual-trigger-deploy-step-to-ci.md:439-440 — NFR findings documented with remediation (expect(Object.keys(workflow.permissions)).toEqual(['actions','contents']) and toContain assertions for each flag) but NOT applied. grep of apps/agent-be/test/unit/deploy-workflow.spec.ts confirms no Object.keys(workflow.permissions) or --status=completed assertions exist. traces/4.6-a1-20260713T093042/prepare-tests.json — --grep for 'permissions|quality-gate|command flag|--status|--limit|--branch|actions.*contents|least-privilege' returns no matches (0 of 221 parts), suggesting the agent did not consider these invariants when creating the guards. Ledger entries 39 (3.8, hypothesized), 48 (3.10, confirmed) record prior occurrences; ledger entry 53 (3.10) records the applied update_step to prepare-tests directing 'apply a uniform guard template to every call site: exercise both credential-isolation invariants and input-injection invariants.'

observations.summary: The review-nfrs step documents MEDIUM+ findings in the story file's review section but does not record them in deferred-work.md, so they persist unresolved in the codebase with no mechanism for a future story to pick them up — the 2 MEDIUM and 6 LOW NFR findings from Story 4.6 are documented but neither fixed nor deferred.
observations.evidence: _bmad-output/test-artifacts/nfr-assessment-4-6.md:37-43 — '5 CONCERNS findings, 0 blockers. All findings are test-coverage gaps in regression guards — the deploy workflow itself is correctly implemented. None block release; all are test-hardening improvements.' Story file _bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/4-6-add-the-manual-trigger-deploy-step-to-ci.md:429-449 — NFR findings documented with [x] checkboxes and remediation recommendations (e.g., 'Add expect(Object.keys(workflow.permissions)).toEqual([actions, contents])', 'Add toContain assertions for each flag'). grep of deploy-workflow.spec.ts confirms remediations NOT applied. grep of deferred-work.md confirms NFR findings NOT recorded — deferred-work.md:423-446 contains only Story 4.6 required reviewers deferral, code review deferrals (9 items from review-code step), and project-context pattern audit deferrals (2 items from update-project-context step); no NFR-specific entries. Playbook review-nfrs prompt: 'Document findings (severity + remediation) in the story's review section' — does not mention deferred-work.md. The update-project-context step (which does write to deferred-work.md) records only patterns, not NFR findings.

amendments.prompt: run Validate mode. Search all test directories in the project and all test file types (including component test files), not just the story's own test files. Flag skipped story-related tests for un-skipping or removal (with reason). For always-skipped tests with no condition that cannot run in any CI tier, remove them directly or convert to conditional skips — do not just flag them, as they persist as dead code across runs when only flagged. Fix stale transitional markers directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories — comments/headers claiming tests are skipped/disabled/red-phase when they're actually active must be updated to reflect current state. Do not defer out-of-scope markers to a separate validation. Treat empty placeholder test stubs (active tests with no assertions, only a comment or empty body) as transitional artifacts — remove them directly wherever found during the search, including those from earlier stories, since an empty test that passes trivially inflates the count without verifying behavior.
amendments.rationale: The prompt says 'flag' for skipped tests, but permanently-dead tests (always-skipped with no condition that cannot run in any CI tier) have persisted across 3 runs (3.3, 3.4, 4.6) because flagging does not lead to action — directing the agent to remove or convert them directly addresses the root cause.

amendments.prompt: run Create mode. Find NFR-specific issues only (e.g., missing select projections, take limits, timing tests, security headers). Document findings (severity + remediation) in the story's review section. For any MEDIUM or higher finding that is not fixed in this step, record it in @_bmad-output/implementation-artifacts/deferred-work.md so a future story whose scope touches the affected code can pick it up.
amendments.rationale: The prompt says to document findings in the story's review section but does not mention deferred-work.md, so MEDIUM+ NFR findings persist unresolved with no mechanism for a future story to pick them up — recording them in deferred-work.md closes the resolution gap.
